title: Data Governance Manager
description:
Your new company

Our client in public sector is looking for a Data Governance Manager to join their team.

Your new role

  • Establish and govern enterprise-wide data policies, standards, catalogs, and metadata to enable secure data use.
  • Lead data stewardship, defining ownership models and coordinating stewards across business domains.
  • Implement data security controls and ensure regulatory compliance, including PDPA and GDPR.
  • Drive secure, reusable data‑sharing practices through curated datasets, contracts, and adoption initiatives.
  • Oversee data asset lifecycle and delivery, coordinating stakeholders and applying Agile methods to ensure high-quality data products.

What you'll need to succeed

  • Degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field with 5+ years of data governance experience.
  • Proven track record implementing data governance or master data management initiatives.
  • Strong skills in data cataloguing, metadata management, data quality, and data modeling.
  • Proficient in SQL, with solid understanding of relational, NoSQL, and modern data architectures.
  • Knowledgeable in data integration, ETL/ELT patterns, data lineage, and data security controls.
  • Strong analytical, documentation, planning, and problem‑solving abilities with ability to work independently.

What you'll get in return

In return, you'll be part of an organization that values its employees. You’ll be rewarded with:

  • Structured career growth and plenty of developmental opportunities
  • Dynamic working environment

What you need to do now

If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' or for more information and a confidential discussion on this role or to find out about more opportunities in Technology, contact Yuki Cheung or email yuki.cheung@hays.com.sg. Referrals are welcome.

At Hays, we value diversity and are passionate about placing people in a role where they can flourish and succeed. We actively encourage people from diverse backgrounds to apply.
